Generated Tauri capability schema; source of truth is upstream Tauri generation.

Static logo asset with embedded image data; user explicitly chose to keep it rather than replace/decompose it.

Single AsyncLocal trajectory state machine whose capture hooks share ordering, parentage, redaction, and a per-run failure circuit; splitting the mutable state across modules would weaken those invariants.

Central streaming and fallback state machine whose model rounds, tool results, emotion state, checkpoints, and B12 context selection share one per-turn closure; source adapters, tokenizer, and assembly policy are already split into dedicated modules, while extracting the remaining live loop would risk cross-round ordering and cancellation invariants.
Historical sequential migration registry for schema versions 1-35; older inline migrations must remain byte-stable while new migrations are already split into dedicated modules.

Security-sensitive versioned wire contract whose exported subschemas, cross-field validation, hostile-input cloning, and redacted diagnostics must evolve atomically so adapters cannot validate against a partially updated manifest definition.

Pure compatibility boundary that coordinates deterministic identity, secret-free adaptation, fail-isolated diagnostics, and cross-source joins for six legacy integration catalogs; splitting the shared traversal would risk inconsistent IDs or partial error isolation.
